title: Designing a Modern NLP Pipeline
concept: A production-grade pipeline: SentencePiece tokenization -> pretrained Transformer backbone -> task-specific adapter layers -> quantized execution with KV caching.

options.text: A storage container for model code.
A sequence of processing steps that text flows through to produce an output.
A type of physical cable used to connect AI servers.

exercises.explain: A pipeline is a sequence of connected steps where the output of one step becomes the input for the next, like an assembly line.

cards.title: AI at Lightning Speed
cards.body: Ever wonder how apps like ChatGPT reply to you almost instantly, even when millions of people are asking questions at the same time? It isn't magic—it's a highly optimized assembly line called a Modern NLP Pipeline. Let's learn how it works!

cards.title: What is a Modern NLP Pipeline?
cards.body:
A modern NLP pipeline is a structured sequence that processes text:
1. SentencePiece splits text into smart sub-word tokens.
2. A pretrained Transformer backbone reads the context and understands the language.
3. Task-specific adapters add custom skills without changing the main model.
4. Quantization and KV caching speed up execution by using smaller numbers and remembering past words.

cards.title: The Fast-Food Assembly Line
cards.body: Imagine building a fast-food kitchen. Instead of slicing fresh bread and grinding meat for every order (slow and custom), you use pre-baked buns (SentencePiece tokens), a high-quality pre-cooked patty (Transformer backbone), add a special sauce for a specific order (Adapters), and wrap it in a compact grab-and-go foil wrapper to keep it warm (Quantization & KV Caching). This assembly line serves customers instantly!

cards.title: The Support Bot Pipeline in Action
cards.body:
Let's look at a customer support bot pipeline:
1. User writes: "I love this app!"
2. SentencePiece splits it: ["I", " love", " this", " app", "!"]
3. Transformer Backbone: Understands English grammar and general meaning.
4. Sentiment Adapter: Classifies the emotion as "Positive".
5. Quantized KV Cache: Generates the reply "Thank you!" super fast using minimal computer memory.

cards.title: Myth: You Must Train the Whole Model
cards.body: Many believe that to make an AI model perform a new task (like medical chat), you have to retrain the entire giant model. In reality, we keep the main "backbone" frozen and only train tiny "adapter" layers. This saves massive amounts of time and money!

cards.title: Old-School vs. Modern NLP
cards.body: See the difference between an unoptimized traditional pipeline and a modern, production-grade NLP pipeline:

left.text: Word-based splitting, training the whole giant model from scratch, and recalculating every single word from the beginning on every step.

right.text: SentencePiece tokenization, frozen Transformer backbone with task-specific adapters, and quantized execution with KV caching.

exercises.prompt: Why do modern NLP pipelines prefer SentencePiece tokenization over simply splitting text by spaces?

options.text: It deletes all punctuation and emojis to save storage space.
It splits text into smart sub-word units, handling new or misspelled words gracefully.
It translates the text into multiple languages automatically.
It runs only after the model has finished generating the final answer.

exercises.explain: SentencePiece handles new or misspelled words easily by breaking them down into smaller sub-word units, preventing the system from failing when it sees an unknown word.

exercises.prompt: KV caching speeds up text generation by storing past word calculations so the model doesn't have to reprocess them.
exercises.explain: KV (Key-Value) caching saves the representations of past words in a conversation so the model doesn't waste time recalculating them for every new word it generates.

exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence explaining how we customize pre-trained models efficiently.
exercises.sentence: To customize a language model for a specific task without retraining the whole backbone, we use task-specific ________.
exercises.explain: Task-specific adapters allow us to customize a large language model for a specific job without retraining its entire massive backbone.

exercises.prompt: How do we train a modern NLP pipeline for a specific task like medical diagnosis?

options.text: We retrain the entire Transformer backbone for every new task.
We keep the Transformer backbone frozen and only train the lightweight adapter layers.
We only train the SentencePiece tokenizer weights for the new task.

exercises.explain: Option B is correct because the backbone is frozen (pretrained), and we only train the lightweight task-specific adapter layers. Option A is incorrect because we do not retrain the entire backbone. Option C is incorrect because SentencePiece is for tokenizing input, not adjusting weights.

exercises.prompt: How do quantization and KV caching work together to make a production NLP pipeline run faster and consume less memory?

options.text: It retrains the Transformer backbone from scratch for every query.
It converts the text to a different human language before processing.
It shrinks model weights to speed up math and stores past context to avoid repetitive calculations.
It disables SentencePiece tokenization entirely to save memory space.

exercises.explain: Quantized execution shrinks the model size and makes math faster, while KV caching stores past conversational history so the model doesn't have to re-read everything from scratch on every new word.
